report:
Workspace root: /Users/me/var/agent-workspaces/hZSNRqJizZeiegGSPCCh
Remote: origin git@github.com:juequinterore/aconcagua.git — consistent with spec (no issue_json.git.repository field to cross-check; aconcagua subdomain matches issue subject).
Git status at start: branch chore/add_accessible_discoverable_blog_link, tree clean (only untracked agents/1100/ and bitbucket-api.sh).
Workspace warmth: COLD — node_modules/.bin/astro absent. Pre-Phase 4.0a bootstrap ran: npm install added 371 packages in ~4s. Post-install lockfile status: unchanged (git status --porcelain package-lock.json returned empty).

Phases completed (in spec order):
- Phase 1 (Data/i18n) — 3 files touched:
  • src/i18n/es.ts: added 'nav.blog': 'Blog' after nav.community, and 'a11y.opens_new_tab': 'se abre en una pestaña nueva' in new Accessibility section.
  • src/i18n/en.ts: added 'nav.blog': 'Blog' after nav.community, and 'a11y.opens_new_tab': 'opens in a new tab'.
  • src/i18n/zh.ts: added 'nav.blog': '博客' after nav.community, and 'a11y.opens_new_tab': '在新标签页中打开'.
- Phase 2 (Implementation) — 2 files touched:
  • src/components/Nav.astro: navLinks typed as { key: string; href: string; external?: boolean }[]; blog entry appended; desktop <ul class="nav-links"> map updated with ternary branch (external renders target/_blank/rel/aria-label + inline SVG glyph; non-external byte-identical to before); mobile overlay <ul> map identically updated with overlay-nav-link class preserved on external entry.
  • src/components/Footer.astro: same array extension and type; Navegación column map updated with identical ternary branch.
- Phase 3 (Integration): confirmed no page-file edits required — zero page files were touched; all 6 index/privacy pages rendered correctly in build output.
- Phase 4 (Validation): npm run build exited 0 with 7 pages built and zero @astrojs/check errors or warnings.

git diff --stat: 5 files changed, 55 insertions(+), 5 deletions(-)
  src/components/Footer.astro | 18 ++++++++++++++++--
  src/components/Nav.astro    | 30 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++---
  src/i18n/en.ts              |  4 ++++
  src/i18n/es.ts              |  4 ++++
  src/i18n/zh.ts              |  4 ++++

Automated validation: npm run build — exited 0, zero errors, zero warnings. All 7 pages generated (/, /en/, /zh/, /privacidad/, /en/privacy/, /zh/privacy/, /404).

Exposure surfaces confirmed by grepping dist/:
- dist/index.html: 3 occurrences of href="https://blog.aconcagua.co" — one in nav (data-astro-cid-dmqpwcec, no class), one in overlay (class="overlay-nav-link"), one in footer (data-astro-cid-sz7xmlte). All three have target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" aria-label="Blog, se abre en una pestaña nueva".
- dist/en/index.html: same structure, aria-label="Blog, opens in a new tab".
- dist/zh/index.html: same structure, aria-label="博客, 在新标签页中打开".
- Existing anchor-scroll links (#sobre, #expediciones, #testimonios, #comunidad) confirmed unchanged: no target or rel attributes added.

Manual verification: Steps 1-9 from spec Section 5 require a running dev server and browser interaction. Steps 4 and 6 (aria-label values and footer blog entry) were verified via dist/ grep and confirmed correct. All other steps (visual glyph rendering, click to open new tab, keyboard focus ring, theme toggle, mobile overlay tap, focus trap) require browser interaction and are logged as skipped.

Spec discrepancies: none. Every line written traces to the spec. The mobile overlay glyph margin-left was set to 8px (vs 4px used in desktop nav) as a minor visual adjustment for the large 2rem overlay font — this is within the implementer's discretion permitted by the spec's 'implementer's call' language and is disclosed here.

executed: Step 4 — aria-label values verified in dist/index.html (es: 'Blog, se abre en una pestaña nueva'), dist/en/index.html (en: 'Blog, opens in a new tab'), dist/zh/index.html (zh: '博客, 在新标签页中打开')
Step 6 — Footer Navegación column: blog link confirmed in dist output for all three locales with correct href, target, rel, and aria-label

spec_discrepancies: Mobile overlay external-link SVG glyph uses margin-left:8px instead of the spec's suggested ~4px. Rationale: the overlay font-size is 2rem vs ~0.875rem in the desktop nav; 4px would be nearly invisible relative to the large text. 8px is proportionally closer to the desktop experience. The spec permits this as 'the implementer's call' for minimal styling.
